user_outcome: Every live release record on the integration branch conforms to the v0.9.0 contract: quadrant 'chore' is migrated to 'quick' AND 'epic' to 'beast' across all releases, in_scope/out_of_scope is present on every spec.json, every board.json drops the now-derived tracks[].state and worktree fields (board-v1 pure plan), the one invalid 'feature' quadrant is fixed, index.md renders are refreshed, and the reader then enforces the v0.9.0 enum only — the data half of sworn#48.
rationale: sworn#48 items 2-3 data half. The issue counted ~28 live records using quadrant 'chore' across 2026-06-30-sworn-operational-readiness, 2026-07-01-release-hygiene, and 2026-07-01-render-drift-reconciliation; this release adds its own (S07 is 'chore' at planning; others are grind/puzzle/epic and unchanged). Backfill policy per the issue: historical specs get empty in_scope/out_of_scope arrays (schema-valid, lazy); this release's 12 specs already carry real content (backfilled at planning, 2026-07-02). The #48 aside is also in scope: one record carries invalid 'quadrant': 'feature' (reported near render-drift's S03-tui-chrome-rework — locate by grep, fix to the correct enum value, note the choice in the journal). FILE-CEILING NOTE: the sweep touches ~40+ record files, over the nominal 15-25 ceiling — accepted as a documented exception because every edit is a one-line mechanical data change from one sweep script; splitting by release would be artificial. DRIFT RISK: in-flight releases (operational-readiness tracks, release-hygiene T1) hold diverged copies of their records on track branches; this migration lands on release/v0.1.0 and reaches those tracks via /implement-slice Step-0 forward-merge self-heal — named here so the drift-gate behaviour is expected, not mysterious (the 2026-06-28 replan-propagation lesson).
in_scope: Quadrant data migration: every '"quadrant": "chore"' becomes 'quick' AND every '"quadrant": "epic"' becomes 'beast' under docs/release/ (spec.json + status.json, all releases including this one)
in_scope/out_of_scope presence backfill: empty arrays for historical specs (driver-contract's 12 already carry real content)
Fixing the single invalid 'quadrant': 'feature' record (the sworn#48 aside)
Tightening state.EffortComplexity.Validate to quick-only (removing S11's transitional chore acceptance)
Re-rendering index.md for every board.json-backed release so the E-by-C column shows the migrated values

board.json pure-plan migration: every docs/release/*/board.json drops tracks[].state, tracks[].worktree, and release.worktree — the fields baton v0.9.0's board-v1 removed as derived; sequenced AFTER S11 lands the deriving code so no binary reads a field that's gone
out_of_scope: Any schema or Go behaviour change beyond removing the transitional tolerance (S11 owns the code half)
Migrating records on in-flight TRACK branches directly — they receive the migration via forward-merge self-heal, never by cross-worktree edits (Rule 11)
board-v1/proof-v1 canonical-shape migration (baton#54, excluded by sworn#48)

effort_complexity.rationale: Mechanical sweep + renders + one tolerance removal; breadth not depth. Runs last in the release so no sibling track holds diverged copies of THIS release's records.

risks.description: In-flight releases' track branches hold diverged record copies; migrating on the integration branch makes those tracks read 'behind'.
risks.mitigation: Expected and self-healing: /implement-slice Step 0 forward-merges release-wt/base into the track. The journal names every in-flight track touched so the next session isn't surprised (replan-propagation lesson, 2026-06-28).

risks.description: A missed record leaves the strict quick-only reader failing on live data after the tolerance is removed.
risks.mitigation: AC-01/AC-03 are grep-proven zero-match assertions run in the same slice that removes the tolerance; the repo-wide doctor/drift check is the backstop.

acceptance_criteria.text: After the migration, `grep -rn '"quadrant": "chore"' docs/release/` SHALL return zero matches, with every migrated record now reading 'quick', and `grep -rn '"quadrant": "epic"' docs/release/` likewise returning zero (all migrated to 'beast'); grind/puzzle untouched (the grep outputs are the slice's reachability artefact, captured in proof.json).

The single record carrying the invalid 'quadrant': 'feature' (located by grep; reported near render-drift S03-tui-chrome-rework) SHALL be corrected to a valid enum value consistent with its effort/complexity axes, with the choice and location recorded in journal.md.

Every spec.json under docs/release/ SHALL contain in_scope and out_of_scope fields (empty arrays permitted for historical records) and SHALL validate against the vendored v0.9.0 spec-v1 schema — proven by a validation sweep whose output is captured in proof.json.
state.EffortComplexity.Validate SHALL accept only quick/grind/puzzle/beast (S11's transitional 'chore' AND 'epic' acceptance removed), and `go test ./internal/state/...` SHALL pass.

When `sworn render` is re-run for each board.json-backed release after the migration, the committed index.md files SHALL match the render output (the fail-closed drift guard passes repo-wide), with the refreshed renders committed in this slice.

After the board.json migration, no docs/release/*/board.json SHALL carry tracks[].state, tracks[].worktree, or release.worktree, and every board.json SHALL validate against the vendored v0.9.0 board-v1 (pure plan: tracks[] = id/slices/depends_on); the validation sweep output is captured in proof.json.
